diagrams.title: Anatomy of a Python Function
diagrams.description: Shows the structural components of a function definition in Python, including the def keyword, function name, parameters, docstring, body, and return statement. This helps learners understand the syntax and purpose of each part.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the basic syntax and components of function definitions in Python

diagrams.title: Methods vs Functions in a Class
diagrams.description: Illustrates the difference between regular functions and methods within a class context, highlighting the 'self' parameter and how instance attributes are accessed. Shows a complete class with both regular functions and methods.
elements: method inside class with self parameter
diagrams.purpose: Distinguishes between functions and methods, explaining the role of 'self' in OOP

diagrams.title: The __init__ Constructor Method
diagrams.description: Visualizes the initialization process of an object using the __init__ method. Shows how parameters passed during object creation flow into the constructor and become instance attributes.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how the constructor initializes objects and sets up instance attributes
